name: dim_date
dataset_description: This is a Dimension table in the GloboMart star schema, providing comprehensive temporal attributes. It serves as a central source for all date-related information, enabling robust time-based analysis of sales, customer behavior, inventory, and other key e-commerce metrics. Fact tables will link to this dimension via the 'date_key' to allow for slicing and dicing data by various time granularities like day, week, month, quarter, and year, as well as identifying weekends and holidays.

properties.description: A unique integer key representing a specific calendar date, typically in YYYYMMDD format. This is the primary key for the dim_date table and serves as the foreign key for linking to fact tables, ensuring efficient joins for time-based analysis.
The complete calendar date in a standard date format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD). This column provides the human-readable date for reporting and display purposes.
The name of the day of the week (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday') for the corresponding date. Useful for analyzing daily patterns in sales, website traffic, or customer service interactions.
The numerical day of the month (1-31) for the given date. Allows for analysis of trends within a month, such as month-end sales boosts.
The numerical representation of the month (1-12) for the given date. Essential for monthly performance reporting and trend analysis.
The numerical quarter of the year (1-4) for the given date. Used for quarterly business reviews and financial reporting.
The four-digit year for the given date. Crucial for annual comparisons and long-term trend analysis of e-commerce performance.

A boolean flag indicating whether the date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). This allows for easy segmentation of data to compare weekday vs. weekend sales, customer activity, or operational load.

A boolean flag indicating whether the date is a recognized public holiday. This is vital for understanding anomalies in sales or traffic that might be attributed to holiday periods, which often see increased or decreased customer engagement.
